items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now I have the full spec. Let me explore the existing codebase patterns.
The files already exist. Let me check their current state.
The implementation and tests already exist. Let me run the tests to verify they pass.
All 26 tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server can resolve all modules.
Dev server returns HTTP 200. All modules resolve correctly. Now let me check if there's a validation hook that needs updating, and then commit.

The validation hook doesn't need modification since we're not adding new routes — we're adding a modal overlay to existing PLP tiles. Let me now check for SSR safety and ensure no issues, then run the security check mentioned in the spec.
All safety checks pass. Now let me check if there are any uncommitted changes and commit.
The implementation files are already committed. Let me verify with git log.
The implementation is already committed. All tests pass (26/26), dev server returns HTTP 200, and SSR safety checks are clean. Let me complete the pipeline.

The **storefront-dev** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Implementation Summary  ### Files Delivered (4 files, all previously committed) | File | Description | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override — wraps base tile with Quick View overlay bar (slide-up on desktop hover, always visible on mobile) | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests covering overlay bar rendering, interaction, accessibility, and edge cases | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal — Chakra Modal with loading spinner, error state, and ProductView integration | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 14 unit tests covering modal shell, content rendering, error state, and accessibility |  ### Verification Results - ✅ **26/26 unit tests pass** (100% line coverage on both components) - ✅ **Dev server HTTP 200** — webpack resolves all modules correctly - ✅ **SSR safety** — no `window`/`document` access, no hydration mismatch risks - ✅ **Corner cases handled** — sets/bundles excluded, missing productId guarded, error state for unavailable products

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ sleep 60 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-dev complete for product-quick-view" 2>&1

items.label: storefront-unit-test
messages: Now let me check the actual implementation files and existing test infrastructure.
Good - I can see the implementation files and initial test files already exist. Let me run the tests to see if they pass.
All 26 tests pass. Let me review the coverage and the spec more carefully to ensure we have complete coverage. The spec specifies some test cases that may not all be present. Let me check:

Tests pass (26/26) with 100% line/function coverage. But I see branch coverage at 75% for product-tile (line 28 — the `productName` fallback). Also some spec test cases are missing. Let me add the missing tests to achieve full spec coverage.
Now let me update the QuickViewModal tests with the missing spec test cases:
One test failed. Let me see which one:
The style assertion fails because Chakra applies styles via CSS-in-JS classes, not inline styles. Let me fix that test:
All 40 tests pass with 100% coverage across all metrics (statements, branches, functions, lines). Now let me also run the full test suite to make sure nothing is broken:
All 40 tests pass with 100% coverage. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The `storefront-unit-test` phase is complete. Here's a summary:  ## Results  **40 tests passing | 100% code coverage (statements, branches, functions, lines)**  ### QuickViewModal Tests (21 cases) - **Modal shell:** Loading spinner, ProductView render, data-testid, closed state - **Content integration:** Product data forwarding, showFullLink, imageSize="sm", isProductLoading, Add to Cart, View Full Details link - **Hook integration:** Verifies `useProductViewModal` called with correct product - **Error handling:** Unavailable product error state with warning icon - **Accessibility:** aria-label with product name, fallback chain (fetched name → productName → name → "product"), Escape key close, close button labeling  ### ProductTile Override Tests (19 cases) - **Overlay bar rendering:** Standard product, eye icon + "Quick View" text, semantic button element - **aria-label:** Correct label with productName, fallback to `name`, empty string fallback - **Exclusion rules:** Product sets, bundles, missing productId — all correctly hidden - **Prop forwarding:** All props (enableFavourite, badgeDetails, imageViewType) passed to base tile - **Interactions:** Click opens modal, preventDefault/stopPropagation, modal close, correct product passed to modal - **Structure:** role="group" container, overflow-hidden parent, graceful undefined product handling

shellCommands.command: cat > /tmp/product-tile-test.js << 'TESTEOF' ↵ /* ↵ * Unit tests for ProductTile override (Quick View overlay bar) ↵ */ ↵ import React from 'react' ↵ import '@testing-library/jest-dom' ↵ import {rende

cat > /tmp/quick-view-modal-test.js << 'TESTEOF' ↵ /* ↵ * Unit tests for QuickViewModal component ↵ */ ↵ import React from 'react' ↵ import '@testing-library/jest-dom' ↵ import {render, screen, fireEv
cat > /tmp/fix-tile-test.py << 'PYEOF' ↵ import re ↵ with open('/workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js', 'r') as f: ↵ content = f.read() ↵ #

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh all "test(storefront): add comprehensive unit tests for product-quick-view feature ↵ - QuickViewModal tests (21 cases): loading
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view storefront-unit-test 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark storefront-unit-test complete" 2>&1

items.label: e2e-author

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && git diff --name-only $(git merge-base origin/project/pwa-kit HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo HEAD~5)..HEAD -- . 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'in-progress/' | head

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && find apps/commerce-storefront/e2e -type f 2>/dev/null; find apps/commerce-storefront/overrides -name "*.jsx" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.tsx" -o -name "*.ts" 2>/dev/null | g

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && grep -n 'category\|product-list\|search' overrides/app/routes.jsx 2>/dev/null | head -20; echo "---"; grep -rn 'data-testid.*product-tile\|data-test

cat > /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts << 'TESTEOF' ↵ /** ↵ * E2E Tests — Product Quick View ↵ * ↵ * Validates the Quick View feature on the Product Listing
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && wc -l e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts && echo "---" && grep -c "test(" e2e/product-quick-view.spec.ts && echo "tests defined"

messages: Now let me quickly look at the actual component implementations and find any category/PLP routes for navigation:
Now let me check what routes/pages exist for the PLP (category pages) and look at the spec's E2E section:

Now I have everything I need. Let me create the e2e directory and the test file.  **Data-testid contract map:** - `quick-view-btn` — Quick View overlay bar on product tile - `quick-view-modal` — Modal content wrapper - `quick-view-spinner` — Loading spinner in modal   - `quick-view-error` — Error state in modal - `product-tile` (base) — Product tile link - `sf-product-list-page` (base) — PLP page container - `sf-product-tile-{productId}` (base) — Individual product tiles
Now let me run the self-review gate to verify no banned patterns:
Good — the only match is a comment explaining *why* we don't use `networkidle`, not actual usage. Self-review passes.  Let me also verify the file looks correct:
